name: Beggar's Chicken
recipeInstructions:
Prepare Chicken: Trim excess fat from chicken; remove wing tips. Mix the mixture for skin and rub it on the inside and outside of the chicken, leaving it uncovered at room temperature to absorb and dry. Repeat if necessary.
Stuffing: In a hot wok, heat peanut oil until smoking. Stir-fry ground pork for 2 minutes, then add mustard green, ginger, and sugar; stir-fry for another minute. Remove and let cool.
Lotus Leaves: The number of lotus leaves needed depends on their size relative to the chicken. Reduce the heat under boiling water to simmer and steep the leaves for 3-5 minutes until they are pliable (no longer or they may shred). Soak in cold water for 10 minutes, or longer, before use; open folded leaves in cold water to wash.
Wrap Chicken: Preheat the oven to 550 degrees Fahrenheit. Stuff chicken with pork mixture. Cut out a central section from the lotus leaves and slit along folds about 3 inches on each side of the center so it can be overlapped. Wrap chicken in three layers of lotus leaves. Tie securely with a piece of cotton or fiber string (avoid nylon as it may melt). Spread wet modeling clay about 1/2 inch thick on newspaper. Place clay-covered chicken around and seal.
Roasting: Place clay-covered chicken on a rack in a shallow roasting pan in the preheated oven and bake for 1 hour. Reduce heat to 350 degrees Fahrenheit and bake for another 1.5 hours.
Serve: Present the fired clay chicken at the table. Crack the clay with a suitable object, allowing some steam to escape before proceeding. Remove the clay; carefully cut open the lotus leaves with scissors. The chicken will be very soft and tender; break apart to reveal the stuffing. Serve guests or allow them to pick with chopsticks.
